events.description:
Make sure to show work (as best as you can) when asked. I suggest doing you work on a scratch sheet of paper and then transfer it onto the computer using equation editor. The equation editor is the square root of x icon (press the three dots icon to view the square root of x icon) and it allows you to use mathematical notation when needed. There is a video below, for more instruction in how to use the equation editor. If the equation editor is not an option, please use your keyboard to write your answers.

You will need a laptop/computer with LockDown Browser to take this exam. A calculator as a cell phone and notes are NOT allowed and please, note that that this exam will be timed. Do not submit the exam until you are completely done answering every question. There will be no re-takes or corrections and the score you receive at the end of the exam is not your final score. There are questions that I need to grade by hand. As a result, these questions will be marked as incorrect.

[How to use Equation Editor.mp4] (https://nmsu.instructure.com/courses/1310474/files/164634111/download?wrap=1)

LAB 14
Nonverbal Communication

Lab Content - Informative Speech Debrief, Nonverbal Communication Activity, Special Occasion Speech Assignment Instructions

Informative Speech Debrief:

● Now that we're finished with informative speeches, what was the most difficult part about
the presentation?
● Now that you know what to expect, how could you better your presentation for your
persuasive speech?
● What do you think you could have done better in the informative speech?
● What advice would you give someone who is presenting for the first time?

events.summary: Grow with Google HSI Career Readiness Program  [COMM-1115G-M3H]
events.description:
--------------------------------------------------------
COMM1115G Grow with Google: HSI Career Readiness Program
--------------------------------------------------------

HSI Career Readiness Program Assignment
---------------------------------------

The GwG program offers five options called “pathways” from which you will complete 2 core lessons and then upload a screenshot to this assignment page. Most lessons include watching a video lesson and then clicking “mark as complete” at the bottom of the screen. For credit purposes, you will need to take a screenshot of the page after clicking “mark as complete” and upload the screenshot as a pdf or JPEG/JPG as proof of completion for credit.  

 

You will find the sign-up instructions that Google has provided below these instructions. This allows you to enroll in the SkillShop LMS so you can access the lessons. You can choose any 2 core lessons you desire. Here is a list of the pathways and core lessons: https://skillshop.exceedlms.com/student/catalog/list?category_ids=6757-hsi-career-readiness-program 

 

Steps to get you started:

1. Read the sign-up instructions (attachment below)

2. Once signed up, click on the Grow with Google Career Readiness Program link in the attachment

3. Watch videos and/or complete exercises in the desired core lesson

4. Click “Mark as Complete” when finished

5. Screenshot and upload proof of completion into this Canvas assignment page.

 

We encourage you to complete at least one core lesson as soon as possible. Please let your GTA know if you have any questions.

Sign-up guide
-------------

[HSI Career Readiness Program sign-up guide.pdf] (https://nmsu.instructure.com/courses/1529739/files/212514447?wrap=1)

Grading
-------

Your completion of the criteria above will gain you the points listed for this assignment. 

If criteria are NOT met you will receive the following point deductions:

* 10% deduction up to 7 consecutive days the assignment is late. After 7 consecutive days, you will receive a zero. 

* 50% deduction for only submitting proof of one lesson.

Your Appointment

events.description: Review coordinate geometry; Practice problems related to reviewed topics; Continue practice and reinforce weak areas
10 push/pull ups ever hour.

This course introduces students to major topics in general biology. This courses focuses on the principles of structure and function of living things at the molecular, cellular and organismic levels of organization. Major topics included are introduction to the scientific process, chemistry of cells, organization of cells, cellular respiration, photosynthesis, cell division, genetics, DNA replication, transcription, and translation. May be repeated up to 1 credit. Prerequisite: MATH 1215 or higher, and a C- or better in (CHEM 1120G or CHEM 1215G or CHEM 1216). Prerequisite/Corequisite: BIOL 2110G.

When the course evaluation for this class opens, please consider taking it!

I value and take your feedback very seriously. Feedback from previous classes have changed the way I teach, so if there's something in this course that really worked for you - or something that didn't - this is your chance to let me know!

I know taking the time to reflect on your classes can seem like one additional thing to do in a week of so many things. Therefore, I can offer you extra credit for completing the course evaluation. I can't see who has turned in the evals, but I can see the percentage of the class who has. 

If 75% of the class completes the course evaluations by Friday, May 12th, I will give the entire class 10 points of extra credit.

I will give you these points in whatever category you need them most: Packback, Exams, Response Papers, wherever. I will assign these points manually by May 14th if earned.

Thanks in advance; you have been a pleasure to have in this course. Many blessings, and have a wonderful break!

events.description: Please don't eat out

Students,

One thing that I have noticed when teaching is that students who organize all of their mathematical material into a notebook have a greater chance of succeeding in the course. As a result, you're expected to keep a notebook or file (if using a tablet/iPad) of all the mathematical material in this course. Especially, the guided notes produced from the video lectures. To ensure that students are keeping up with the video lectures, notes and other course material. 

In this assignment you will be uploading your notes that have been produced from viewing the video lectures. Your notes can be summitted through a video, scanned image, or photo'd using your cell phone or device. If you have a iPad/Tablet you may show work by using your stylus/iPencil and upload your document with your answers.

I will be focusing on the completion and organization of your notes; have you written down all key concepts, principals, definitions and example problems in clear manner. Remember, these notes serve as a resource to help you succeed so it's important that your notes are eligible.

This Progress Report is the first part of the Individual Project, due later in the semester.

Click for more details on the Individual Project: [Individual Projects - Instructions] (https://nmsu.instructure.com/courses/1525373/pages/individual-projects-instructions)

Progress Report:
----------------

Write and submit a ½ page paper depicting where you are in your project. Please answer the following:

* Cite the two sources you are planning to use. APA or MLA format. (4 points)

* Explain your progress so far on the project. (2 points)

* How does your project answer the main question of the assignment? Why or how did you arrive at these decisions? (4 points)

* Attach any work-in-progress so far.

Submit your work here on Canvas. No emailed or hard copies will be accepted.

Total Points possible: 10 (+5 extra credit for work-in-progress)

10 minutes*, 10 questions, 10 points. Good luck!

(*unless previously arranged)

-------------------------------------
COMM 1115GLab 15: Paper/Podcast Prep 

-------------------------------------

Lab 15
Podcast/Paper Prep

Lab Content - Paper/Podcast Assignment Prep, Peer Review Activity.

Paper/Podcast Assignment Prep: 

We will spend at least 15 minutes reviewing the assignment guidelines AND completing our introductions for EITHER the paper or podcast - be prepared to work on it.

Peer Review Activity:

We will work in small groups to prepare & present our introductions & give each other constructive feedback. 

HOMEWORK:

Reminder: the paper OR podcast is due SUNDAY, March 12th,  before 11:59 pm through
Canvas. If you have any trouble submitting your work, please email it to me directly.

[Screen Shot 2022-10-03 at 3.38.48 PM.png] (https://nmsu.instructure.com/courses/1525373/files/212599329/preview)

Welcome to your midterm exam for THEA 111!

Please read the following information carefully before beginning the exam.

* We do not have in-person class today. The exam is all you need to focus on.

* This exam is worth a total of 30 points. 

* The questions are a total of 20 questions, which are a mix of multiple choice, matching, true-false, and short answer. There are a few extra credit questions scattered within as well.

* The short-answer questions and the extra credit will be graded manually over the next week.

* There is a time limit of 2 hours. Once you begin the exam you must finish within 2 hours. There are no additional attempts.

* You are allowed to use your notes.

* Because you are allowed to use them, spelling does count. This includes but is not limited to any names, definitions, places, terminology, etc.

* Take your time! Read over each question thoroughly before you answer.
[Links to an external site.] (https://nmsu.zoom.us/j/9309695670)

* Although I will be checking email throughout the day, especially during class time, please be aware that I do not often check email after business hours. This means that if you are taking the exam last-minute, I cannot guarantee I will be able to respond fast enough to help you should you have a question on the exam.

2 hours is plenty of time to take and finish the exam. Let me know if you have questions before you start. Good luck!

OPTIONAL: THEA 111 - Third Play Response (EXTRA CREDIT)
(Any Live Theatre Performance, in addition to Hookman and Godspell)

I highly recommend writing and submitting your response paper as soon after viewing as possible so you don't forget anything!

~*~

* This response paper, if fully completed, will serve as extra credit.

* For extra credit, you may view an additional play or musical and write an additional response paper.

* Your additional play/musical must be approved by Professor Hermanson in advance.

* The response paper must fulfill all requirements of other response papers in this class. Completed papers will be worth up to 60 points. 

* Your paper must follow the same guidelines as the required response papers: a minimum of 750 words, and you will need to use one MLA cited source. All papers must be turned in using Packback Deep Dives, which can be found in the sidebar on the left. No other form of submission will be accepted.

~*~

COMM 1115GLab 17: Special Occasion Speech Viewing
-------------------------------------------------

------
Lab 17
------

Special Occasion Presentations

Lab Content - Special Occasion Presentations:

Each of you needs to submit a recording no later than the night before this lab. You will need to upload it to our section “Studio” on Canvas, if possible OR to this Module. This way all the recordings can be in one spot (or two) and I can run through them smoothly. During lab, I will be playing each of these videos submitted in class and grading them accordingly using the rubric on Canvas. 

This should be fun & good practice for the Persuasive Speech Assignment. Please email me directly if you have any questions or concerns!

 

Reminder:

Next week is Exam 2! It will be the same format as Exam 1, online through Canvas. It will be open Wednesday, March  29th. It is a timed assessment so you will only have 50 minutes to complete it once you begin. It is 40 questions (T/F & multiple choice) open book, open note. We will review for the Exam in Lab 18. Please study the material & be prepared to play some Jeopardy!

This course will introduce students to the concepts, theories, significant findings, methodologies, and terminology that apply to the field of psychology.

Limits and continuity, theory and computation of derivatives, applications of derivatives, extreme values, critical points, derivative tests, L'Hopital's Rule. This is an Honors version of MATH 1511G. It is taught with MATH 1511G with differentiated assignments. Prerequisite: Adequate scoring on the Mathematics Placement Exam, or any ACT/SAT and GPA combination that is considered equivalent, or a C- or better in MATH 1250G or higher.

This course introduces students to major topics in general biology. This courses focuses on the principles of structure and function of living things at the molecular, cellular and organismic levels of organization. Major topics included are introduction to the scientific process, chemistry of cells, organization of cells, cellular respiration, photosynthesis, cell division, DNA replication, transcription, and translation. Must be taken with BIOL 2110L to meet general education requirements. May be repeated up to 3 credits. Prerequisite/Corequisite: a C- or better in MATH 1215 or higher and a C- or better in (CHEM 1120G or CHEM 1215G or CHEM 1216).

Temple Appointment (Proxy Endowment) 30 Dec 2022

Confirmation number:
Review basic algebraic concepts; Review trigonometric functions; Review exponential and logarithmic functions

Students,

One thing that I have noticed when teaching is that students who organize all of their mathematical material into a notebook have a greater chance of succeeding in the course. As a result, you're expected to keep a notebook or file (if using a tablet/iPad) of all the mathematical material in this course. Especially, the guided notes produced from the video lectures. To ensure that students are keeping up with the video lectures, notes and other course material. 

In this assignment you will be uploading your notes that have been produced from viewing the video lectures. Your notes can be summitted through a video, scanned image, or photo'd using your cell phone or device. If you have a iPad/Tablet you may show work by using your stylus/iPencil and upload your document with your answers.

I will be focusing on the completion and organization of your notes; have you written down all key concepts, principals, definitions and example problems in clear manner. Remember, these notes serve as a resource to help you succeed so it's important that your notes are eligible.
Exponential and logarithmic functions; Advanced differentiation and optimization; Applications of integration (continued)
